Participation

Events & Activities

🌍 31 March – 3 April 2026 · Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

IPORA SAEAP 2026 Annual Scientific Conference

Participated in the IPORA SAEAP 2026 Annual Scientific Conference hosted at Addis Ababa University. The conference, organized within the IPORA Network (Strengthening Africa-Europe Academic Partnership for Improving Intersectoral Governance), brought together researchers, students, and institutional partners from across Africa and Europe to discuss interdisciplinary solutions at the intersection of health, environment, food systems, and society.

As part of the Food and Nutrition Security session, contributed to:
📡 “Adoption and Diffusion Assessment from the Sky”
🌿 “Mapping Diffusion of Improved Fodder Using EO and Machine Learning” — case study from the Awash Sub-Basin, Ethiopia.

Session: Innovations for Food and Nutrition Security
Chaired by Dr. Paulos Getachew (AAU)

• Adoption/diffusion remote sensing — Dr. Michael Marshall & Dr. Mariana Belgiu (Univ. Twente), Binyam Tesfaw, Mintesinot Berhanu, Samuel Getachew (AAU)
• Afar forage case study — Prof. Kaleab Baye, Dr. Binyam Tesfaw, Mintesinot Berhanu, Samuel Getachew (AAU)
• Synthesis & policy implications — Alemayehu Seyoum Taffesse (IFPRI)
IPORASAEAP2026Earth ObservationMachine LearningFood SecurityRemote SensingInterdisciplinary

📊 30 March – 3 April 2026 · Addis Ababa, Ethiopia

ViSTA Ethiopia Data Sprint 2026 — Advanced Analytics for Maize Yield & Product Performance

Participated in the ViSTA (Varietal Intelligence for Seed Targeting and Adoption) Ethiopia Data Sprint, a five-day intensive workshop advancing secure, actionable analytics for maize yield and product performance. The sprint, coordinated by Resourced Solutions and led by EIAR, integrated Ethiopia’s ground-based agricultural datasets with emerging AI and Earth Observation (EO) technologies.

The sprint demonstrated that EO-based maize crop mapping at national scale is feasible using Sentinel-2 imagery and ML workflows (OlmoEarth platform), achieving 80.4% accuracy in maize distribution maps for 2017 and 2022. Key contributions included crop mapping pipeline development, yield estimation modelling using SHAP-based feature analysis, and policy brief preparation.

Partners: EIAR · Ethiopian Statistical Service · Addis Ababa University · Bahir Dar University · Allen Institute for AI · CIMMYT · FAO/GPSDD · Systems & Technology Research

ViSTAMaize MappingEO AnalyticsSentinel-2MLFood SystemsCGIAR
🛸 Certificate Earned

🎓 2025 · Holeta Agricultural Research Center, Ethiopia

Training Workshop: Satellite-Based Crop & Water Monitoring (CropWatch / ETWatch)

Completed a hands-on training workshop on CropWatch and ETWatch technologies for agricultural monitoring using satellite data and Earth Observation. Field training at Holeta HARC connected satellite observations with real-world crop health, water monitoring, and pest management conditions. Organised by UNCTAD, ANSO, AIR-CAS, EIAR, ATI, and Ambo University.

CropWatchETWatchSatellite MonitoringClimate-Smart AgricultureDigital AgricultureGIS

📚 Starting August 2025 · Live via Zoom & In-Class · Ethiopia

SpatialWise Applied Geospatial Webinar Series — Premium Training Program

Launched and led the SpatialWise Applied Geospatial Webinar Series — a professional training program delivering cutting-edge skills in GIS, Remote Sensing, LiDAR, Python, MATLAB, R, Machine Learning, and GeoAI to professionals and students across Ethiopia and Africa.

Participants learn to process and analyze satellite imagery, extract features from LiDAR point clouds, automate spatial workflows using Python, apply machine learning for land use and vegetation analysis, and master geodetic techniques for high-precision mapping. Sessions are live via Zoom and in-class, with materials and certificate of completion provided. Fee per session: 1,000–1,500 Birr.

📧 minteb11@gmail.com  ·  📲 @Minteb11  ·  t.me/spatialwise  ·  Register Here

Background

My Story

Growing up in Ethiopia, I witnessed how fragile agricultural systems are when confronted with environmental change. That observation became a lifelong question: can the view from space help us understand, protect, and sustainably manage the landscapes that feed us?

Over more than a decade, I have built expertise at the intersection of Earth observation, machine learning, and agricultural science — from lecturing GIS and remote sensing at Mettu and Wollega Universities, to leading research as a PhD candidate at Addis Ababa University’s School of Earth Sciences. My doctoral work integrates multi-sensor satellite data (Sentinel-1 SAR, Sentinel-2, Landsat, LiDAR GEDI) with AI-driven pipelines to forecast crop yields and assess nutritional outcomes in smallholder farming landscapes.

Today I contribute to international collaborations including the ViSTA initiative (multi-country crop mapping and yield analytics) and the CGIAR SPIA ADD-IN Ethiopia project. I founded SpatialWise — a geospatial training and consulting platform — to transfer these skills to the next generation of African spatial scientists.

Research Output

Publications

🏆 Muna MedLife Award for Best Paper — Mediterranean Life Science Union Annual Meeting (MedLIFE-25)

Naples, Italy · 1–11 December 2025 · Paper No. 123

Integrating Satellite and Strategic Ground-Based Monitoring for Flood Risk Assessment and to Support Public Health Mitigation Measures in Resource-Limited, Data-Scarce Settings

Berhanu, M., & Perilli, N. · MedLife Conference 2025, Naples, Italy · Presented Dec 2025

Integrates satellite-based flood monitoring with ground survey data to support public health decision-making in low-resource environments. Awarded Best Paper at MedLIFE-25.

→ Conference Program

Rainfall Anomalies During the Dry Season and Their Impact on Rainfed Crop Yields in Ethiopia

Tamsgen, A., Berhanu, M., et al. · Journal of Hydrology: Regional Studies · Submitted 2025 (MS: EJRH-D-25-03363)

Analysis of dry-season rainfall anomalies and their downstream effects on rainfed crop productivity across Ethiopian agricultural zones, combining satellite-derived precipitation products with yield statistics.

Wetland Mapping and Evaluating the Impacts on Hydrology Using Geospatial Techniques: A Case of Geba Watershed, Southwest Ethiopia

Berhanu, M., Suryabhagavan, K.V., & Korme, T. · Geology, Ecology, and Landscapes · 2021

Geospatial analysis of wetland extent and hydrological impacts in the Geba Watershed using multi-temporal satellite imagery and GIS-based spatial modelling.

→ View Paper

African Civet Habitat Mapping in Illubabor Zone, Southwest Ethiopia: A Geospatial Approach

Suryabhagavan, K.V., Berhanu, M., et al. · Remote Sensing of Land, 5(1) · 2021

Species habitat suitability mapping for the African Civet using satellite-derived environmental covariates and geospatial modelling across Southwest Ethiopia.

→ View Paper

Mapping and Estimating Aboveground Biomass Using Random Forest Algorithm: A Case of Yayo Bioreserve Forest, Southwestern Ethiopia

Berhanu, M. · Submitted

Machine learning-based biomass estimation integrating multi-temporal satellite imagery with field inventory data across the Yayo Biosphere Reserve, Southwest Ethiopia.

Work

Projects

🛸

Improved Fodder Diffusion Mapping – Awash Sub-Basin

IPORA SAEAP 2026 featured research: mapping diffusion of improved fodder species using Earth Observation and ML, case study from Ethiopia’s pastoral Awash Sub-Basin.

ViSTA Maize Crop Mapping & Yield Estimation

National-scale maize crop mapping using Sentinel-2 and OlmoEarth ML platform achieving 80.4% accuracy; yield estimation via SHAP-based feature analysis across Ethiopian smallholder zones.

ADD-IN Ethiopia – CGIAR SPIA

Examining adoption and diffusion of CGIAR agricultural innovations using nationally representative datasets and geospatial analytics. SPIA Add-In Ethiopia Project Winner (AAU).

Ethiopian River Network Geovisualization
